text: Women, Infants, & Children (WIC) | Placer County, CA Skip to Main Content Home Departments Departments E - L Health and Human Services Divisions Public Health Women, Infants, & Children (WIC) Women, Infants, & Children (WIC) WIC is a Supplemental Nutrition Program to help families eat well, be active and stay healthy.   WIC's goal is to improve the health of families during critical times of growth and development. WIC services include: Nutrition Information - Information about nutrition and health to help families eat well and be healthy. Breastfeeding Support - New and expecting moms receive information and resources to get them ready to feed their new babies. Includes access to lactation experts. WIC Card - A special electronic benefit card (EBT)  to buy healthy foods such as fruits and vegetables, whole grains, milk, eggs, cereal, peanut butter, beans, soy milk, tofu and more. ***NEW*** Visit the California WIC website https://myfamily.wic.ca.gov/Home/YourWICFoods to learn about fresh updates to WIC foods as of April 13, 2026. WIC has added more healthy options for all children and adults such as canned fish, fresh cut herbs, and expanded choices for plant-based milk alternatives, yogurt flavors, and whole grain options. More food package sizes have been added to make shopping easier. New Foods include: All fresh-cut herbs such as parsley, mint, and cilantro; whole grain corn masa flour, whole wheat bagels, English muffins and pita bread, and many new whole grain breakfast cereals; peach, blueberry, mango. and strawberry-banana flavors of yogurt (depending upon brand); Silk Kids Original Oatmilk Blend and various Ripple brands of pea protein milk alternatives;   canned chunk light tuna, pink salmon, and sardines packed in water, mustard, or tomato sauce.  New package sizes of cheeses and whole grain products have been approved. Referrals - WIC can help families find a healthcare provider and provide referrals to other social services such as Medi-Cal, CalWorks, CalFresh and more, depending on individual needs. Do I Qualify? Where is WIC? What do I bring? What should I expect? Do I Qualify? Do I Qualify? Check to see if you are eligible for the WIC Program. https://myfamily.wic.ca.gov/Home/AmIEligible Eligibility is based on income and being in one of the following categories below: A pregnant woman A woman breastfeeding a baby under 1 year of age A woman who had a baby or was pregnant in the past 6 months A baby up to his or her first birthday A child up to his or her fifth birthday You may qualify if you receive Medi-Cal, CalWORKs (TANF) or CalFresh (SNAP) benefits. Babies and children cared for by their fathers, other guardians, or foster parents are also eligible. Working families and migrant families are also welcome to apply. Apply Here for benefits online. Where is WIC? WIC Locations Please contact the WIC Program by calling 916-784-6447 or 530-889-7187, or by emailing the WIC program at [email protected] . The WIC staff will give you an enrollment appointment and directions to the nearest office. At your appointment, WIC staff will check to see if you and your family qualify. All persons enrolling in WIC will need to be present at the enrollment appointment. You can also visit one of the following office locations for more information: Auburn HHS Center WIC Office Kings Beach WIC Office Lincoln WIC Office Rocklin County of Placer Sunset WIC Office What do I bring? What do I bring to my first WIC appointment? Bring the following for each person who will be enrolling into the WIC program Identification - for yourself and any children age birth to 5 years Proof of address - a document verifying your current address Proof of income - current documents (within the previous 30 days) such as a paycheck, tax return, or letter from employer. What should I expect? What should I expect at my first WIC appointment? A WIC counselor will determine if you are eligible for the program and will provide a review of the WIC program services & benefits. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G_9w2X1AMqM&list=PLuBcbwDEv8PwocWEWrQpdA7QFqqxaISfs&index=1 A Nutrition Assessment will also be completed for for each qualifying member of your family. WIC food benefits for all qualifying members of the family will be loaded onto the California WIC card. to purchase healthy foods. Find more information about shopping the WIC Card at myfamily.wic.ca.gov .      Apply for Services New to WIC Enrolled in WIC Apply for Services If you are pregnant or have a child under age 5 and need assistance, please Apply for Services . Men, dads, grandparents, foster parents, and caretakers are all welcome to apply for WIC benefits for their children under age 5. The WIC screening process can be completed by phone. We will set up an appointment to enroll you, and you will receive WIC benefits the day you qualify. Please call (916) 784-6447 our office or click on the Apply for Services link above. New to WIC Welcome to WIC Review the links below to learn about what WIC has to offer. 1.  Welcome to WIC English video (21:09) Welcome to WIC (English) 2.  Welcome to WIC Spanish video (25:07) Welcome to WIC (Spanish) Visit the California WIC You Tube Channel for the Welcome to WIC video captioned in Arabic, Armenian, Chinese, Dari, Farsi, Hmong, Korean, Pashto, Punjabi, Russian, and Vietnamese. 3.  Fruits and Vegetables (1:43) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=orQwFP8H4tM 4.  Foods for Infants and Moms who Breastfeed (3:53) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1gL-KLa2cuo 5.  California WIC Card (8:50) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gz_j2hkvb2c&list=PLuBcbwDEv8PzQnHlz2WOicc_KenGaQmXu&index=6&t=0s 6.  Know Your Rights and Responsibilities (7:02) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fhDcUHcaMKY Enrolled in WIC Appointments In order to serve you and continue to provide your benefits in a safe, efficient, and effective manner, we ask that you do the following: If you DO NOT have an appointment, please call us at 916 784-6447 or email us at [email protected] If you have MISSED your appointment, please call us at 916 784-6447 or email us at [email protected] If you have a SCHEDULED in-person appointment, please arrive 5 minutes early.  For SCHEUDLED phone appointments be available around the time of your appointment to receive a call from your WIC counselor. The call may come from a blocked phone number. In addition, make sure to bring in or email any required documents we have asked you to bring in. If you are emailing documents, please do so a few hours before your appointment. Your Benefits May Be Available Benefits may be automatically added to your WIC card. Please use the California WIC App or call 844.469.3264 to check your food balance. If benefits HAVE BEEN added to your card: Review your benefits balance to ensure it looks correct. Review your benefits start and end dates, as this may have changed. We will be in contact with you to schedule your next appointment. If your benefit balance looks incorrect, or if you need to make changes to your foods, please call us at 916 784-6447. If everything looks correct, there is no need to follow up, and you can begin using your benefits as soon as they are available to you. If benefits HAVE NOT been added to your card: We will provide your benefits during your next scheduled appointment. If you don’t have a scheduled appointment, please call us at 916 784-6447 or email us at [email protected] If you have not yet received your WIC card, we can mail it to you Call us at 916 784-6447 or email us at [email protected] with your name, mailing address, and date of birth to request your WIC card by mail. We will notify you by phone or email when your card has been mailed and you can expect to receive it within 5 business days. Visit www.myfamily.wic.ca.gov to watch a video about the new WIC card.
